name: Addition of SID History to Active Directory Object
description: Detects the addition of SID History attributes to an Active Directory object. This behavior can be used by an attacker to inherit additional privileges from a compromised account, often for privilege escalation or persistence.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the Domain Controller **{hostname}**: the **SID History** attribute was added to the Active Directory object for user **{target_user_name}**. This highly suspicious activity, performed by **{subject_user_name}**, is a known technique for **Privilege Escalation** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1134.005**) and **Persistence**. It allows an attacker to inherit the privileges of another account without needing its password. Immediate action is required to investigate the event ID **{event_id}** and determine the legitimacy of this change. This event should be treated as a confirmed security incident until proven otherwise.

parameters.name: target_user_name
parameters.description: The name of the Active Directory object to which SID History was added.

parameters.name: subject_user_name
parameters.description: The user account that performed the modification.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Windows Security Event ID (e.g., 4765, 4766, or 4738).

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname of the Domain Controller where the event occurred.
